title: Crispy Algerian Dates Pilaf
description: A fresh and chewy Algerian dish featuring molasses, broiled dates as a diabetic-friendly side dish for solo dinners.
instructions: Prepare dates by removing pits and soaking in water for about 30 minutes.
Drain dates and pat dry with paper towels. Slice each date into quarters if large or halves if small.
In a large bowl, combine olive oil, cumin seeds, salt, molasses, and turmeric powder. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly distributed.
Add sliced dates to the dressing mixture and toss gently to coat all pieces thoroughly.
Place coated dates on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Broil in the preheated oven for 5-7 minutes or until dates turn golden brown and crispy.
Remove from heat and let sit for about 2 minutes before serving.
